<div class="sphinxpress-doc">
<section id="text2epub-documentation">
<h1>text2epub documentation</h1>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">text2epub</span></code> is a typed Python package for creating EPUB files from text-first
workflows and for safely rebuilding existing EPUB packages from structured
extraction manifests.</p>
<p>The general-purpose creation workflow supports single Markdown files, folders of
ordered Markdown chapters, explicit chapter lists, and already-rendered XHTML
bodies. The rebuild workflow remains conservative for automation tools such as
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">booktx</span></code>, where preserving unchanged ZIP entries and failing closed on stale
inputs matters.</p>

<section id="supported-workflows">
<h2>Supported workflows</h2>
<ul class="simple">
<li><p>Generate a new EPUB from one Markdown file.</p></li>
<li><p>Generate a new EPUB from a folder of Markdown chapters sorted by filename.</p></li>
<li><p>Add generated title and reader-visible contents pages to Markdown or XHTML builds.</p></li>
<li><p>Request automatic TOC page numbers for reading systems that support CSS paged-media counters.</p></li>
<li><p>Discover chapters from manuscript naming schemes such as <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">00-front-matter.md</span></code>,
<code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">01-introduction.md</span></code>, and <code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">02-chapter.md</span></code>.</p></li>
<li><p>Build an EPUB from already-rendered XHTML chapter bodies.</p></li>
<li><p>Rebuild an existing EPUB from a manifest plus replacement JSON.</p></li>
<li><p>Validate the basic ZIP/package structure and scan text entries for unresolved
internal tokens.</p></li>
</ul>
</section>
<section id="design-priorities">
<h2>Design priorities</h2>
<ol class="arabic simple">
<li><p>Make the common Markdown-to-EPUB path easy for general Python users.</p></li>
<li><p>Keep output deterministic by default so builds are reproducible.</p></li>
<li><p>Preserve source EPUB bytes for no-op and identity rebuilds.</p></li>
<li><p>Fail closed when rebuild inputs are stale or unsafe.</p></li>
<li><p>Keep generated front matter optional so scripts can choose plain chapter-only output or book-like output.</p></li>
<li><p>Keep the public API small enough for automation tools and scripts.</p></li>
</ol>
</section>
<section id="limits">
<h2>Limits</h2>
<p><code class="docutils literal notranslate"><span class="pre">text2epub</span> <span class="pre">validate</span></code> is a package-level smoke check. It does not replace
EPUBCheck and does not perform full EPUB specification validation of OPF, NAV,
NCX, XHTML, CSS, or remote-resource policy.</p>
</section>
</section>
